inclusions: Heading 88.01 — Balloons and dirigibles; gliders, hang-gliders and other non-powered aircraft

Heading 88.02 — Other aircraft (e.g., helicopters, aeroplanes); spacecraft (including satellites) and suborbital and spacecraft launch vehicles. Subheadings 8802.11–8802.40 classified by UNLADEN WEIGHT (weight of machine in normal flying order, excluding crew, fuel, and non-permanently fitted equipment)
Heading 88.03 — Parts of goods of heading 88.01 or 88.02
Heading 88.04 — Parachutes (including dirigible parachutes and paragliders) and rotochutes; parts thereof and accessories thereto
Heading 88.05 — Aircraft launching gear; deck-arrestor or similar gear; ground flying trainers; parts of the foregoing articles

Heading 88.06 — Unmanned aircraft. Subheadings 8806.21–8806.24 and 8806.91–8806.94 classified by MAXIMUM TAKE-OFF WEIGHT (weight at take-off including payload, equipment and fuel). Includes drones designed to carry a payload OR equipped with permanently integrated digital cameras or other equipment for utilitarian functions during flight

exclusions.condition: Flying toys designed SOLELY for amusement purposes
exclusions.action: Redirect to heading 95.03 (toys) — Note 1 para 2. Even if the toy flies or resembles a drone, if designed solely for amusement → 95.03. Key test: UTILITARIAN FUNCTION vs. AMUSEMENT ONLY

classification_rules.rule: Note 1 — Definition of 'unmanned aircraft' (UAV/drone) for heading 88.06
classification_rules.description: An 'unmanned aircraft' (88.06) is ANY aircraft (other than 88.01 non-powered aircraft) designed to fly WITHOUT a pilot on board. Two qualifying types: (1) designed to carry a payload; (2) equipped with permanently integrated digital cameras or other equipment enabling utilitarian functions during flight. EXCLUDES: flying toys designed solely for amusement (→ 95.03). The 'utilitarian function' test is decisive: aerial photography for commercial/professional use, surveying, delivery drones → 88.06; simple toy that flies → 95.03.

For classifying aircraft in subheadings 8802.11 to 8802.40 (aeroplanes, helicopters by weight band), 'unladen weight' = weight of machine in NORMAL FLYING ORDER, EXCLUDING: (a) weight of crew; (b) weight of fuel; (c) weight of equipment other than permanently fitted items. Only permanently integrated equipment (fixed instrumentation, avionics, built-in cameras) counts toward unladen weight. Removable payload does NOT count.

classification_rules.rule: Subheading Note 2 — 'Maximum take-off weight' (MTOW) for subheadings 8806.21–8806.24 and 8806.91–8806.94
classification_rules.description: For classifying unmanned aircraft in heading 88.06 by weight band, 'maximum take-off weight' = maximum weight at take-off INCLUDING: (a) payload; (b) equipment; (c) fuel. This is the opposite philosophy from unladen weight: MTOW is the heaviest the aircraft can operate at departure. Weight bands in 88.06 subheadings are determined by MTOW as declared by the manufacturer.

The single exclusion from Ch.88 for unmanned aircraft is flying toys designed SOLELY for amusement. Classification decision tree: (1) Does it fly without a pilot? Yes → potential 88.06. (2) Is it designed SOLELY for amusement? Yes → 95.03. (3) Does it carry payload OR have permanently integrated camera/sensor for utilitarian function? Yes → 88.06. Borderline cases (e.g., toy drone with removable camera) → apply PRINCIPAL USE test: professional/commercial aerial photography capability → 88.06; basic consumer toy → 95.03.

classification_rules.rule: Key boundary: 88.01 (non-powered aircraft) vs 88.02 (powered aircraft) vs 88.06 (unmanned aircraft)
classification_rules.description: Three headings by propulsion and pilot status: 88.01 = NON-POWERED, piloted (gliders, hang-gliders, balloons, dirigibles). 88.02 = POWERED, piloted (aeroplanes, helicopters, spacecraft, satellites). 88.06 = UNMANNED (no pilot), any propulsion type — BUT heading 88.06 specifically carves out of 88.01 by Note 1 language ('other than those of heading 88.01'), meaning unpowered unmanned aircraft (e.g., unpowered UAV gliders) could fall in 88.01 rather than 88.06.

classification_rules.rule: Parts of aircraft: 88.03 vs component-specific headings
classification_rules.description: Heading 88.03 covers parts of goods of headings 88.01 and 88.02 (NOT 88.06 parts — those are in 88.06 itself or 88.03 by general parts rule). General rule: parts that are identifiable as being solely or principally for aircraft → 88.03. Parts that are general-purpose and not exclusively aircraft-specific may be classified in their own heading (e.g., aircraft engines → 84.11 turbojets/turbopropellers; aircraft tyres → 40.11). This follows the general Section XVII parts principle.
